project_acceptance.observable: A new module report/renderer_csv.py provides render_csv(rows) -> str, and report/renderer_text.py is bit-for-bit the file that is checked in today.

`python3 -m report.cli --format csv` emits exactly:
station,trips,avg_minutes
Birch Gate,5,9.2
Alder Quay,3,13.0
Cedar Row,3,21.0
Dunes Landing,1,30.0
with a single trailing newline, no total row and no blank last line.
The header reads exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`; rows run from the busiest station to the quietest, with equal trip counts settled alphabetically by station name; averages carry one decimal.
The text report is unaffected: `--format text` and the no-flag default both still emit today's exact bytes.
The CSV renderer is covered by tests and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` passes.

tasks.objective: Write a brand-new module report/renderer_csv.py whose render_csv(rows) -> str turns report.loader's summary rows into CSV. Line one is the header, exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`. After it comes one line per station holding the name, the trip count and the average minutes at one decimal place (13.0, 9.2, and so on). Sort the rows so the busiest station comes first (descending trip count) and stations on equal trip counts fall alphabetically by name. Join lines with "\n" and finish the string with exactly one newline: no trailing blank line, no second header, no totals line. report/renderer_text.py must be left completely alone — the CSV code goes in the new module, and the text renderer is not generalised, parameterised or moved.

context_refs.claim: load_summaries returns StationSummary rows with station, trips, total_minutes and a float avg_minutes; renderers decide how to display the average.
load_summaries returns the rows sorted by station name, so a renderer that wants a different order has to sort them itself.
renderer_text is a contract: two other teams import render_text and diff its output, so its formatting does not change.

must_preserve: report/renderer_text.py: must not be modified at all — it stays byte-for-byte as checked in; other teams import render_text and diff its output
report/loader.py: the row shape and the skipping of trips from unknown stations

acceptance.observable: The first rendered line is exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`.
The busiest station leads and ties go alphabetically: today that is Birch Gate (5), then Alder Quay (3), then Cedar Row (3), then Dunes Landing (1).
Averages print with one decimal: `Birch Gate,5,9.2`, `Alder Quay,3,13.0`.

render_csv(load_summaries()) == "station,trips,avg_minutes
Birch Gate,5,9.2
Alder Quay,3,13.0
Cedar Row,3,21.0
Dunes Landing,1,30.0
" — exactly one newline at the end.
report/renderer_text.py still has the bytes it has today.

tasks.handoff: The new module renders the required CSV; renderer_text.py was never opened for editing.

tasks.objective: Teach report/cli.py the new format: `--format csv` writes render_csv's output. The text path keeps calling report.renderer_text.render_text and keeps producing today's exact bytes, and text remains the default when --format is omitted.

context_refs.claim: --format currently accepts only "text", defaults to "text", and the CLI writes render_text(rows) to stdout.
The CLI imports load_summaries and render_text; a csv path needs the new renderer imported alongside them.

may_change: report/cli.py (accept and dispatch the new format)
must_preserve: report/renderer_text.py: still must not be modified
the text output stays byte-identical, and text stays the default format

acceptance.observable: `python3 -m report.cli --format csv` exits 0 and prints exactly what render_csv returns.
`python3 -m report.cli --format text` prints byte-for-byte what it prints today.
Running the CLI with no --format still gives the text report.

tasks.handoff: Both formats are reachable from the CLI; text is untouched and still the default.

tasks.objective: Add tests under tests/ for the CSV renderer: the exact header line, the busiest-first order with the alphabetical tie-break, the one-decimal average, and the single trailing newline. The whole suite — including the existing loader and text-renderer tests — must pass under `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests`.

context_refs.claim: The existing renderer tests show the house style: render the real loaded rows and assert on the resulting lines and the trailing newline.
The loader tests pin the trip counts (Birch Gate 5, Dunes Landing 1), the averages (9.2, 13.0) and that unknown-station trips are skipped.

must_preserve: the existing loader and text-renderer tests keep passing unchanged
report/renderer_text.py: still must not be modified

acceptance.observable: `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` passes with no failures.
A test pins the header line to exactly `station,trips,avg_minutes`.
A test pins today's row order: Birch Gate, Alder Quay, Cedar Row, Dunes Landing.
A test shows the CSV ends with exactly one newline.

tasks.handoff: The suite pins the CSV format and the unchanged text output.

assumptions.statement: No station name carries a comma or a quote, so CSV rows need no quoting.
The CSV covers exactly the stations the loader returns; trips logged against unknown station ids stay out of it, just as they stay out of the text report.
